documents.name: Insurance Policy Card / E-Card
documents.notes: Patient must carry this at all times during hospitalisation. Without it, the hospital insurance desk cannot verify eligibility.

documents.name: Photo ID (Aadhaar / PAN / Passport)
documents.notes: Carry ID for both patient and policyholder if they are different persons.

documents.name: Doctor's Prescription
documents.notes: OPD prescriptions leading to hospitalisation are claimable under pre-hospitalisation expenses.

documents.name: Lab / Investigation Reports
documents.notes: Collect originals from every department individually — lab, radiology, pathology. Hospitals do not volunteer these at discharge.

documents.name: Admission Advice Note
critical_fields: Treating doctor name and registration number
documents.notes: This is the trigger document for any insurance claim. Without it, neither cashless nor reimbursement can be initiated. The most critical single document in the entire process.

documents.name: Pre-Authorisation Request Form
documents.notes: Filed by hospital, not by patient. Patient should confirm with hospital insurance desk that this has been submitted.

documents.name: Pre-Auth Approval Letter (AL)
documents.notes: Patient should request a copy. Check: does the sanctioned amount cover the estimated bill? If not, an enhancement will be needed.

documents.name: Enhancement Request Form
documents.notes: Filed when treatment cost exceeds sanctioned amount. Common for extended stays, complications, or additional procedures.

documents.name: Payment Receipts
documents.notes: Every payment — deposit, interim, pharmacy, final — must have a stamped original receipt. These are mandatory for reimbursement claims.

documents.name: Discharge Summary
critical_fields: Treating doctor name and registration number
documents.notes: Second most critical document after admission advice. Do not leave the hospital without it. Must be signed by the treating doctor, not just a junior resident.

documents.name: Final Hospital Bill (Itemised)
documents.notes: Must be itemised, not just a total. Itemisation is needed for TPA to verify charges against policy sub-limits. Ask specifically for itemised breakup if hospital offers only a summary.

documents.name: Pharmacy Bills (Stamped)
documents.notes: Must match entries in the treatment chart and discharge summary. External pharmacy bills (non-hospital) should also be stamped and accompanied by the corresponding prescription.

documents.name: Reimbursement Claim Form
documents.notes: Must be submitted with all original documents within 15-30 days of discharge (check policy for exact deadline). Incomplete forms are the single most common reason for reimbursement delays.

documents.name: Settlement Letter
documents.notes: If the approved amount is less than claimed, the deduction reasons must be itemised. This document is the basis for any grievance filing if the patient disagrees with the settlement.

documents.name: Follow-up Consultation Bills
documents.notes: Claimable within the post-hospitalisation window (typically 60-90 days, check policy). Must be accompanied by the prescription and should reference the original hospitalisation.
